meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Overcome Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how a SaaS starter kit helps you overcome debugging struggles. A beginner-level tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary barrier for students building their first SaaS. By utilizing a high-quality SaaS starter kit, you eliminate boilerplate errors and configuration drift. Combined with AI-driven agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a struggling coder into an architect who solves logic gaps instantly.
content.intro:
You stare at the terminal, blinking cursor mocking your progress. A sea of red stack traces fills your screen, and the frustration of a broken dependency or a misconfigured environment feels like a wall you cannot climb. You aren't just stuck; you're experiencing the rite of passage every senior engineer has survived: the realization that manual debugging is a bottleneck to your vision.

But what if you didn't have to debug the plumbing? By leveraging a proven SaaS starter kit, you bypass the architecture traps that snare most beginners. We’re going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development environment into an autonomous powerhouse, allowing you to focus on building features that matter instead of chasing syntax errors.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Production-Grade Starter Kit
steps.description: Don't build from scratch. Use a starter kit to get authentication, database ORM, and Stripe integration pre-configured, reducing the surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the boilerplate template. Ensure all environment variables are structured for local development and production readiness.

steps.title: Configure the Intelligent Context Engine
steps.description: Establish a clear project context so your AI tools understand your folder structure and dependency graph before you start coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the current project directory and generate a comprehensive context map for the codebase, identifying the core dependencies and auth flow.

steps.title: Automated Bug Analysis
steps.description: Instead of manual debugging, pipe your error logs directly into Claude Code to get an architectural explanation of the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the latest crash logs in the terminal. Identify the root cause, suggest a fix, and explain the architectural implication of the solution.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Coding Patterns
steps.description: Use your AI agents to enforce type safety and error handling across your new API endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current API route and implement defensive error handling. Add meaningful logging and ensure all edge cases for database queries are wrapped in try-catch blocks.

steps.title: Validate and Deploy
steps.description: Run a final automated test suite and push your code to your hosting provider using the starter kit's built-in CI/CD pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a sanity check on the latest feature implementation. Run the test suite and confirm the code complies with the project's style guide.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm test && npm run deploy'

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data, not failures; feed them into your AI agent to build a 'knowledge base' of common project pitfalls.
Always use a starter kit that includes built-in unit testing; it catches 90% of 'can't debug' scenarios before they reach the browser.
Keep your Claude Code context window clean by periodically clearing old, irrelevant logs to maintain high-quality AI reasoning.
Focus on modularity; if a feature is hard to debug, it's likely too large. Ask your AI to refactor the feature into smaller, testable functions.
Document your AI-assisted fixes in a simple README file to build a portfolio that showcases your ability to leverage modern tooling.
resources: SaaS Starter Kit Best Practices: https://github.com/saas-starter-kit/guide
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, begin adding custom business logic. Use the 'Refactor' command in Claude Code to optimize your code for performance as your user base grows.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start mastering the architecture. You have the tools and the guide to build your first professional SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
